modules.name: models
modules.responsibility: Define the Todo domain entity as a plain @dataclass. No to_dict / from_dict methods — serialization belongs to the repository layer, not the domain. Entity fields are the sole source of truth for schema.
interfaces: @dataclass class Todo: id: str; title: str; completed: bool; created_at: str

modules.name: repository
modules.responsibility: Define the TodoRepository Protocol and provide FileRepository (JSON-backed) and InMemoryRepository (for tests). Owns all dict<->Todo conversion using dataclasses.asdict() for writes and Todo(**data) for reads. No module outside repository touches serialization.
interfaces: class TodoRepository(Protocol): def list(self) -> list[Todo]: ...; def add(self, todo: Todo) -> None: ...; def remove(self, id: str) -> None: ...; def update(self, todo: Todo) -> None: ...
class InMemoryRepository(TodoRepository): __init__(self, todos: list[Todo] | None = None) -> None
injection_points: TodoRepository is injected into TodoService; tests inject InMemoryRepository — no filesystem required

modules.name: service
modules.responsibility: All business logic: add, list_all, complete, remove. Shared prefix-lookup extracted to _resolve_by_prefix(id_prefix) to eliminate duplication between complete() and remove(). Each public method has CC≤3.
interfaces: class TodoService: def __init__(self, repo: TodoRepository) -> None: ...
def _resolve_by_prefix(self, id_prefix: str) -> Todo: raises ValueError on 0 or >1 match
injection_points: TodoRepository injected at construction; tests pass InMemoryRepository

modules.name: cli
modules.responsibility: Parse CLI arguments with argparse and delegate to per-command handler functions. main() is a thin dispatcher: build parser → parse args → call handler → return exit code. Each handler is a module-level function accepting (service, args) — independently testable without going through argparse. CC of main() ≤3.
interfaces: def main(argv: list[str] | None = None, service: TodoService | None = None) -> int: ...
injection_points: TodoService injected into main() for CLI-level integration tests without filesystem

modules.name: config
modules.responsibility: Resolve the data file path from env var TODO_FILE or platform default (~/.todos.json). Single pure function, no side effects — tests override via env.

fields.name: todos
fields.note: serialized via dataclasses.asdict(); ordered by created_at

ux_flows.name: add_todo
ux_flows.success_criteria: Todo appears in list output immediately after add
error_states: Empty title → stderr: title cannot be empty, exit 1
File write fails → stderr: could not save todos, exit 1

ux_flows.name: list_todos
ux_flows.success_criteria: All todos shown with id, status, title, date in ≤1s
error_states: No todos → prints: No todos yet
File read fails → stderr: could not load todos, exit 1

ux_flows.name: complete_todo
error_states: ID not found → stderr: no todo with id abc123, exit 1
Ambiguous prefix → stderr: prefix matches N todos, be more specific, exit 1

ux_flows.name: remove_todo
error_states: ID not found → stderr: no todo with id abc123, exit 1

customer_journey: The user installs the package (pip install todo-cli), runs 'todo add "task"' to capture something fast, 'todo list' to review, 'todo complete <id>' when done, 'todo remove <id>' to clean up. Todos survive across shell sessions because they persist to ~/.todos.json. The entire task lifecycle takes ≤3 commands. No config needed for basic use.

experience_metric.name: task_lifecycle_in_3_commands
experience_metric.target: Add, complete, and remove a todo in ≤3 commands, each completing in ≤0.5s on a laptop
experience_metric.measurement: Time each CLI invocation; verify file persists between process restarts

architecture_decisions.title: Repository Protocol for storage abstraction
architecture_decisions.context: Sprint-1 benchmark showed 0% test coverage. All I/O was inline in business logic, making unit testing impossible without patching the filesystem.
architecture_decisions.decision: Define a TodoRepository Protocol. FileRepository is the production implementation. Tests inject an InMemoryRepository — no mocking required.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Protocols over ABC: lighter, structural, no inheritance needed. Eliminates all mock/patch usage in tests.
consequences: Cyclomatic complexity of service methods drops because each method does one thing
Tests run without touching the filesystem — fast and deterministic
Adding a SQLite or cloud backend is a new Repository class, not a service change

architecture_decisions.title: JSON file as persistence format with schema versioning
architecture_decisions.context: todo-cli is a trivial complexity project. A database adds infra dependency that exceeds the complexity budget.
architecture_decisions.decision: Persist as JSON: {"version": 1, "todos": [...]}. Version field enables future migration without breaking existing files.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Human-readable, no dependencies, trivially testable with temp files.
consequences: Not suitable for concurrent writes from multiple processes — accepted for single-user CLI
Migration logic lives in FileRepository, not in models

architecture_decisions.title: CLI injectability for integration testing
architecture_decisions.context: Sprint-1 found 0 tests passing. The CLI called service directly without any seam for testing.
architecture_decisions.decision: main() accepts optional service: TodoService parameter. When None, it constructs the real service with FileRepository. Integration tests pass a service backed by InMemoryRepository.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Keeps CLI testable without filesystem while keeping the default path zero-config for end users.
consequences: main() signature has an optional parameter — callers not passing it are unaffected

architecture_decisions.title: Drop Serializable Protocol — repository owns serialization
architecture_decisions.context: Friction 75f7d625: 10 duplicate code blocks detected in models.py. The Serializable Protocol (to_dict / from_dict) forces every domain entity to manually implement boilerplate serialization, duplicating 6+ lines per entity.
architecture_decisions.decision: Remove the Serializable Protocol entirely. The repository layer uses dataclasses.asdict() for writes and Todo(**data) for reads. Models are pure @dataclass with no serialization methods.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Domain entities should express business invariants, not serialization format. The repository already controls the file format — it is the natural owner of dict<->entity conversion.
consequences: All serialization is co-located in repository.py — one place to update if the format changes
New entities require no to_dict / from_dict implementation
models.py has no imports from infrastructure — cleaner domain boundary

architecture_decisions.title: CLI command handler decomposition — handlers as named functions
architecture_decisions.context: Friction b6d8fba6: cli.main() has CC ~10 and spans 56 lines. All four command branches, three try/except blocks, and output formatting are inlined in a single function.
architecture_decisions.decision: The cli module interface must expose four module-level handler functions: _handle_add, _handle_list, _handle_complete, _handle_remove. Each accepts (service: TodoService, args: argparse.Namespace) -> None. main() becomes a thin dispatcher. CC of main() ≤3; CC of each handler ≤3.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Named handlers are independently testable without argparse. Decomposition enforced at design level guarantees Build agent cannot produce a monolithic main().
consequences: main() drops from 56 lines to ≤15 lines
Each command can be unit-tested with a mock service and a Namespace, no subprocess required
Adding a new command means adding a new handler function — not modifying the dispatcher body

architecture_decisions.title: Service prefix-resolution extracted to _resolve_by_prefix helper
architecture_decisions.context: Friction 9f7f1aa2: complete() and remove() share an identical 5-line prefix-resolution block. The design v4 did not name this shared logic, so Build agent duplicated it.
architecture_decisions.decision: The service module interface explicitly includes _resolve_by_prefix(self, id_prefix: str) -> Todo as a named private method. It raises ValueError on zero matches and on multiple matches.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Naming the shared behavior at design level guarantees the Build agent extracts it. The helper has a single invariant to maintain — centralizing it means a future change to error messaging is a one-line edit.
consequences: complete() and remove() each reduce to 2 lines of action code after resolution

threat_model.description: Malicious todo title containing shell metacharacters if title ever passed to subprocess
threat_model.mitigation: Never pass todo content to subprocess. Output is formatted via f-strings to terminal only.

threat_model.description: Another process modifying ~/.todos.json between read and write (TOCTOU)
threat_model.mitigation: Accepted risk for single-user CLI. Concurrent writes from two processes may lose data.

threat_model.description: Corrupted JSON file causes unhandled crash on startup
threat_model.mitigation: FileRepository wraps json.load in try/except; on parse failure backs up the file and starts fresh with an empty store.

privacy_inventory.retention: Until user removes the todo or deletes the file
privacy_inventory.encryption: At rest: none (plaintext in ~/.todos.json). At transit: N/A — local only.

i18n_plan.note: All user-visible strings are in handler functions in cli.py. Extract to a strings dict if i18n becomes required.

open_questions: Should completed todos be hidden from list by default, or shown with a strikethrough style?
Should todo remove require confirmation for incomplete todos?

sections.title: Module Boundaries
sections.content: 5 modules: models (plain @dataclass, no serialization), repository (TodoRepository Protocol + FileRepository using dataclasses.asdict() + InMemoryRepository), service (business logic + _resolve_by_prefix helper, injected repo), cli (main() dispatcher + 4 named handler functions, injected service), config (data path resolution).

sections.title: Data Model
sections.content: Todo: id (UUID4), title (str ≤256), completed (bool), created_at (ISO UTC). Persisted as JSON {version, todos[]}. Serialization lives in repository via dataclasses.asdict().

sections.title: UX Flows
sections.content: 4 flows: add_todo, list_todos, complete_todo, remove_todo. Each ≤4 steps. All dispatch through named CLI handler functions. Error states are explicit.

sections.title: Architecture Decisions
sections.content: 6 ADRs accepted: ADR-001 TodoRepository Protocol, ADR-002 JSON persistence with versioning, ADR-003 CLI main() injectable service, ADR-004 Drop Serializable Protocol (serialization→repository), ADR-005 CLI handler decomposition (main CC≤3), ADR-006 _resolve_by_prefix service helper.

sections.title: Threat Model
sections.content: 3 STRIDE entries: T (shell injection — mitigated), I (TOCTOU — accepted), I (corrupt JSON — mitigated).
